title: corydalis griffithii subsp. griffithii
intro: Corydalis griffithii subsp. griffithii, also known as Griffith's corydalis or Himalayan corydalis, is a plant species within the corydalis genus that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and allocryptopine. The plant has various health benefits, including pain relief, reducing inflammation, and potentially treating conditions such as anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Corydalis griffithii subsp. griffithii is often combined with other herbs, such as ginseng and licorice root, and is typically consumed as a tea, tincture, or capsule.
